subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Escape Velocity and Gravity
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Escape Velocity and Gravity.

hook.text: Every object has an escape velocity—the speed needed to break free of its gravity. Earth's is 11.2 km/s.

buildup.text: The more massive and compact an object, the higher its escape velocity. Neutron stars need 100,000 km/s.

discovery.text: A black hole's escape velocity exceeds the speed of light. Since nothing travels faster, nothing escapes.

twist.text: This means a black hole doesn't suck things in. It traps things that get too close—like a cosmic pitfall.

climax.text: If the Sun became a black hole, Earth wouldn't be sucked in. It would keep orbiting—just in total darkness.

punchline.text: Not a vacuum cleaner. A trap for anything that crosses the line.

quiz.question: What would happen to Earth's orbit if the Sun became a black hole of the same mass?
options: Earth would continue orbiting normally since the gravitational pull stays the same
Earth would be sucked into the black hole immediately
Earth would fly off into space without the Sun's light
